sources_note: Water math mirrors the EcoLogits methodology (ecologits.ai): energy is estimated per model, then converted to water with WCF = E_server x [WUE_onsite + PUE x WUE_offsite] (Li et al. 2025). Per-model energy is calibrated to the 'How Hungry is AI?' benchmark of 30 models (arXiv:2505.09598), cross-checked against Altman 2025 (0.34 Wh/query), Google Gemini 2025 (0.24 Wh / 0.26 mL), UC Riverside (arXiv:2304.03271), and Epoch AI. Every number is an estimate; the three modes are the honest range, not a single truth.

energy._note: Facility-level energy in Wh (PUE already included). Per request: fixed_wh + (input_tokens/1000)*input + (output_tokens/1000)*output. Calibrated so a short GPT-4o query (100 in / 300 out) ~= 0.34 Wh and a long one (10k in / 1.5k out) ~= 1.8 Wh, matching the benchmark.

param_fallback._note: EcoLogits GPU energy model for models NOT in the table above: f_E(P,B) = alpha * e^(beta*B) * P_active + gamma, Wh per output token, B = batch size. Multiply by system_overhead to reach facility energy, then *1000 for Wh/1k. P_active in billions of ACTIVE parameters (dense: total; MoE: total / active experts).

water._note: WCF per mode. E in kWh; factors are L/kWh (== mL/Wh). conservative = on-site cooling only (Scope 1, what companies report ~10-15% of total). standard = + electricity-generation water (Scope 2, ~70-85%). full = + embodied/lifecycle share (Scope 3).
